'''Modified Strike Cruisers''' were [[Loronar Corporation/Legends|Loronar Corporation]] [[Strike-class medium cruiser|''Strike''-class medium cruisers]] modified by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] to carry [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projectors]].

==History==
When the Rebel Alliance built stolen gravity well projectors into Loronar ''Strike''-class [[Cruiser/Legends|cruisers]], they called the result a "Modified Strike Cruiser" for lack of a better designation. The fuselage of this particular modified version of the ''Strike''-class cruiser had been altered to carry gravity well projectors in a pair of bulbous external housings. With these, the ship could be used as an [[Interdictor/Legends|interdictor]] similar to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]].

The first Modified Strike Cruiser was [[Peregrine (Strike-class)|''Peregrine'']], which was refitted by the Rebel Alliance and their [[Airam (group)|Airam]] allies with gravity well projectors from the captured Interdictor cruiser ''[[Compellor]]''. At least one other ship, ''[[Sagina]]'', was built to this design at [[Telgorn shipyard|shipyard]] ''[[Calenz]]'' in the [[Nocto system/Legends|Nocto system]], but it is unknown if it already carried operational gravity well projectors when the shipyard was destroyed.

After the [[Spreading the Rebellion|Airam campaign]], there were no recorded sightings of this class. The Rebellion evidently lost the interest and/or capability to build such ships when their shipyard ''Calenz'' was destroyed. Instead, they eventually went on to use the [[CC-7700 frigate/Legends|CC-7700 frigate]] and growing numbers of captured Immobilizer 418 cruisers.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing vs. TIE Fighter: Balance of Power Campaigns]]'' {{1st}}
